evals.name: budget-monthly-categories
evals.prompt: Quiero agregar presupuestos mensuales por categoría a la app de mis finanzas. Antes de ponernos a codear, ayúdame a dejar el spec bien definido.
evals.expected_output: Creates docs/specs/<date>-<slug>/requirements.md from the template with numbered EARS acceptance criteria in English, then pauses for approval before design.md.
assertions: requirements.md is created inside a date-stamped folder docs/specs/2026-07-02-<slug>/
Acceptance criteria use EARS notation (WHEN / IF / THEN / WHILE / WHERE combined with SHALL)
Requirements and their criteria are numbered and traceable (e.g. Requirement 1, criteria 1.1, 1.2)
Contains user stories in the form 'As a <role>, I want <capability>, so that <benefit>'
Includes at least one error / unwanted-behavior criterion (IF ... THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL ...)
Requirements content is written in English
Respected the approval gate: paused for user approval after requirements before creating design.md
design.md (produced after simulated approval) has architecture, data models, error handling and testing-strategy sections that trace back to requirement numbers

evals.name: expense-auto-categorization
evals.prompt: Necesito definir cómo va a funcionar la categorización automática de gastos según el nombre del comercio. Escribamos primero las requirements.
evals.expected_output: Requirements-first: requirements.md with user stories + EARS criteria covering happy and error paths, numbered and traceable, in the dated specs folder.
assertions: requirements.md is created inside a date-stamped folder docs/specs/2026-07-02-<slug>/
Acceptance criteria use EARS notation (WHEN / IF / THEN / WHILE / WHERE combined with SHALL)
Requirements and their criteria are numbered and traceable (e.g. Requirement 1, criteria 1.1, 1.2)
Contains user stories in the form 'As a <role>, I want <capability>, so that <benefit>'
Includes at least one error / unwanted-behavior criterion (IF ... THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL ...)
Requirements content is written in English
Respected the approval gate: paused for user approval after requirements before creating design.md
design.md (produced after simulated approval) has architecture, data models, error handling and testing-strategy sections that trace back to requirement numbers

evals.name: csv-bank-import
evals.prompt: Quiero specar una feature para importar movimientos bancarios desde un CSV que exporta mi banco.
evals.expected_output: Asks a few sharp clarifying questions when the idea is vague, then produces requirements.md in EARS with out-of-scope and open-questions sections.
assertions: requirements.md is created inside a date-stamped folder docs/specs/2026-07-02-<slug>/
Acceptance criteria use EARS notation (WHEN / IF / THEN / WHILE / WHERE combined with SHALL)
Requirements and their criteria are numbered and traceable (e.g. Requirement 1, criteria 1.1, 1.2)
Contains user stories in the form 'As a <role>, I want <capability>, so that <benefit>'
Includes at least one error / unwanted-behavior criterion (IF ... THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL ...)
Surfaced sharp clarifying questions before committing to requirements (the prompt is intentionally vague)
Uses the Out of scope and/or Open questions sections from the template
Respected the approval gate: paused for user approval after requirements before creating design.md
